agent_notes: City of London is unusually strong for historic drinks rooms and small museums, but weak for PAINTS Books & Coffee: I found bookshops and cafes, but no in-district independent bookshop-plus-coffee venue that clearly supports an 8-12 person two-hour stay.

No Creative Walks included: the district does not contain a PAINTS-approved park, and the closest permitted non-park riverside route is South Bank to Tate Modern / Bankside rather than a City of London route.
Most qualifying venues sit in the single Central / City cluster; this necessarily breaches the cross-London 35% balance rule because this file is district-only.
Google ratings/review counts, live walk-in tolerance for groups of 8-12, and July 2026 date-specific availability must be host-confirmed before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Official site confirms wine bar/shop/importer; The Nudge listing states Mon-Fri 11:00-23:00 and Sat 17:00-23:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm current July 2026 hours and any private-hire closure.
venues.walk_in_policy: The Nudge says visitors can email to book or just turn up; group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely workable only early weekday or with heads-up because the candlelit vaults are intimate; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.capacity_notes: Cellar wine bar under Holborn Viaduct; private-hire risk exists.
venues.why_fits_paints: An atmospheric vaulted natural/organic wine importer with a real small-producer point of view. It gives guests bottles, producers, arches and City history to talk about without feeling like a corporate wine bar.
venues.anecdote_story: RAW WINE describes it as born in the Italian countryside and established in the City, with over 15 years supporting small organic and biodynamic producers.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Organic, biodynamic and small-producer wines; bar/shop format.
venues.safety_notes: Busy central route between Farringdon, Blackfriars and St Paul's; use main streets for late exits.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk via Holborn Viaduct / Fleet Street - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small venue; group walk-in and Saturday opening need direct confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: CAMRA lists Tue 14:00-22:00, Wed-Fri 12:00-22:00, closed Sat-Sun-Mon. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm current July 2026 hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Micropub format implies walk-in drinking, but no explicit group policy found; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Operationally fragile: CAMRA describes it as very small and busy with about 20 drinkers.
venues.capacity_notes: Tiny one-room micropub; best for 8 rather than 12.
venues.why_fits_paints: A tiny independent City pub with a strict London-made drinks identity. It is more craft-beer than natural-wine, so it stays Tier B, but the local-fermented premise gives a strong show-and-tell hook.
venues.anecdote_story: OneCity says everything served is London brewed, fermented or distilled; CAMRA notes new management from December 2025 and a changed beer selection.
venues.serve_or_on_view: London craft beers and London-made spirits; changing keg lines.
venues.safety_notes: Bank/Throgmorton Street is well lit and high-footfall on weekdays; weekend closure limits late-night risk.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 7 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Does not strictly meet the v6 natural-wine/gastro-pub drinks requirement; very limited capacity.

venues.opening_hours: Official contact page lists Monday 11:00-22:00 and Tuesday-Friday 11:00-23:00; Saturday/Sunday not shown in listing snippet. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub/wine-bar service appears walk-in-led; group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible early weekday, but likely busy after work.
venues.capacity_notes: Ground-floor bar plus downstairs restaurant; operator Shepherd Neame keeps it from Tier A under PAINTS chain caution.
venues.why_fits_paints: A hidden alley wine house with enough coffee-house history to carry a whole conversation. The venue is obvious to City pub historians but still feels tucked away to casual visitors.
venues.anecdote_story: The venue presents itself as the site of London's first coffee house, opened in 1652, now a Grade II listed pub/wine bar known as the Jampot.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wine, beer, pub food and City coffee-house history.
venues.safety_notes: Central Bank/Monument alleys are safe by day and early evening; host should steer attendees to Monument or Bank after dark.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 6 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Operator is a brewery/pub group and TripAdvisor score surfaced below PAINTS Tier A floor; use as Tier B only.

venues.opening_hours: Londonist lists Mon 12:00-22:00, Tue-Fri 12:00-23:00, closed Sat-Sun; official Davy's page confirms venue identity. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm July 2026 hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Wine bar/pub format suggests walk-ins, but explicit group policy not found; UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely possible at off-peak times with prior call; not a spontaneous Friday after-work pick.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic wine bar with dining/private-event spaces; private hire can affect availability.
venues.why_fits_paints: The room has City age, low-lit wine-cellar energy and a genuine survival story. It is capped at Tier B because Davy's is a small group operator rather than a fully independent one.
venues.anecdote_story: Davy's and Londonist describe it as the City's oldest wine house, built in 1663 and among the buildings that survived the Great Fire.
venues.safety_notes: Cannon Street/Monument area is well connected; avoid dispersing guests through empty alleys late at night.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 6 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small-chain operator and weekday-only pattern limit PAINTS spontaneity.

venues.opening_hours: FT 2025 listing states Mon-Fri 12:00-22:00; official Davy's page confirms the venue. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm July 2026 hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Wine bar format likely accepts walk-ins, but group of 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Possible only off peak; wooden seating and narrow space reduce two-hour comfort.
venues.capacity_notes: Historic Fleet Street wine bar; small-chain Davy's ownership caps score.
venues.why_fits_paints: Good journalist-era Fleet Street story and serious enough wine list. It is less off-grid and more obvious than the best PAINTS drinks venues, so it stays Tier B.
venues.anecdote_story: History Hit and FT note its Fleet Street journalism associations and origins as a wine merchant founded in 1879.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Wines by the glass, tapas and European small plates.
venues.safety_notes: Fleet Street and Blackfriars are safe and transit-rich; best for weekday daylight or early evening.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Chain/operator penalty and only moderately comfortable for a long seated group.

venues.opening_hours: Official Nicholson's page lists daily pub hours in search result; Londonist gives historic hours. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm July 2026 hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub format likely accepts walk-ins; group of 8-12 and table availability are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Can work if hosted early, but chain pub peak times are risky.
venues.capacity_notes: Large enough compared with many City pubs; Nicholson's chain ownership caps at Tier B.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Arts and Crafts / Art Nouveau interior is genuinely showable. It fails the independent-drinks ideal, but the sculptural room is strong enough as a Tier B cultural drinks backup.
venues.anecdote_story: The pub is Grade II* listed, remodelled circa 1905, and was reportedly saved from demolition in the 1960s after campaigning associated with John Betjeman.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Traditional pub drinks and food; sculptural friar-themed interior.
venues.safety_notes: Directly by Blackfriars station with high-footfall routes.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 8 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not independent; can feel touristy and crowded.

venues.opening_hours: Official Urban Pubs page lists Mon-Wed 11:00-23:30, Thu-Fri 11:00-00:00, Sat 11:00-00:00, Sun 12:00-22:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm bank-holiday/private-hire status.
venues.walk_in_policy: Pub format likely accepts walk-ins; group table policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Feasible off peak; Friday after-work is high risk.
venues.capacity_notes: Bigger than micropubs; group operator and category looseness keep it Tier B.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Fleet Street satire link gives the host an easy social object. It is more heritage pub than PAINTS wine room, so use only as a robust backup.
venues.anecdote_story: The pub was renamed in the 1840s because Punch magazine had offices nearby; the magazine's staff are part of the venue lore.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks and food; Punch magazine / Fleet Street history.
venues.safety_notes: Fleet Street is well lit with City Thameslink and Blackfriars nearby.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Urban Pubs group venue; PAINTS fit depends on using the history, not the drinks list.

venues.opening_hours: Official site lists Mon-Sat 12:00-23:00 and Sun 12:00-22:30. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm July 2026 and private-event status.
venues.walk_in_policy: Historic pub format likely walk-in-led; group table policy UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Works only if host arrives early; low light, staircases and compartmentalized rooms can split the group.
venues.capacity_notes: Multiple rooms but awkward circulation; inaccessible for wheelchairs per official site.
venues.why_fits_paints: A literary, low-lit Fleet Street institution that gives guests immediate prompts. It is very obvious and Samuel Smith-operated, so it is a backup rather than a PAINTS-perfect pick.
venues.anecdote_story: Rebuilt after the Great Fire of 1666, associated with Dickens, Twain and the preserved parrot Polly in venue lore.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Traditional pub drinks/food; historic rooms, cellars and literary associations.
venues.safety_notes: Safe central route; accessibility and stairs are the bigger operational concern.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 4 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Obvious, crowded, inaccessible and chain-operated; use for story density rather than intimacy.

venues.opening_hours: Barbican official listing shows Project a Black Planet running Thu 11 Jun-Sun 6 Sep 2026; event-date ticket availability must be confirmed.
venues.walk_in_policy: Timed-entry/ticketed exhibition; walk-in not guaranteed. Host must confirm availability for group date.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if tickets are booked or timed-entry availability exists.
venues.capacity_notes: Major art-gallery infrastructure; booking and wayfinding are main operational issues.
venues.why_fits_paints: The current exhibition is the draw, not just the institution: over 300 works on Pan-Africanism across painting, installation, posters, journals and film. It gives strangers an unusually rich shared vocabulary for identity, archives and image culture.
venues.anecdote_story: The Barbican's brutalist estate context adds a second layer: a cultural complex inside the Square Mile that can feel like its own city.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Project a Black Planet: The Art and Culture of Panafrica, 11 Jun-6 Sep 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Safe daytime route from Barbican/Moorgate; host should pre-plan the meeting point because Barbican wayfinding is famously confusing.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 10 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Timed-entry capacity and Barbican wayfinding; Guardian review is mixed, so host should frame it as discussion-rich rather than universally loved.

venues.opening_hours: City of London pages confirm gallery/whats-on pages; current listings include Underground (and Surface) Exhibition 27 Feb-20 Sep 2026 and Heritage Gallery Display: America and London 2 May-29 Oct 2026. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm daily opening hours/date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free gallery; official page says no booking required for daily individual tours, but groups are excluded from those tours. Group entry tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for self-guided 8-12 if host does not rely on public tours.
venues.capacity_notes: Museum/gallery setting with Roman amphitheatre remains below; no group tour without arrangement.
venues.why_fits_paints: A genuinely City-specific art collection with a Roman amphitheatre in the basement. The 2026 Underground / Surface and America-and-London displays give the host grounded prompts rather than generic gallery browsing.
venues.anecdote_story: The gallery is owned by the City of London Corporation and sits beside Guildhall, connecting civic power, Victorian collecting and Roman Londinium.
venues.serve_or_on_view: City art collection, Roman amphitheatre, Underground (and Surface), America and London Heritage Gallery display.
venues.safety_notes: Guildhall Yard/Bank is safe in daytime; City gets quieter at weekends.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 7 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Opening-hours and group-tour details require direct confirmation.

venues.opening_hours: Official page lists Tue-Sat 10:00-18:00, Sun 12:00-17:00, first Thu until 20:00, closed Mondays and Christmas/New Year bank holidays.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free entry but time-slot model appears common; group walk-in/booking tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if timed slots are secured; limited capacity makes spontaneous walk-in risky.
venues.capacity_notes: Immersive archaeological display under Bloomberg; small timed experience.
venues.why_fits_paints: It pairs Roman ritual archaeology with contemporary art programming in the most literal under-layer of the City. The sensory temple reconstruction is memorable without relying on blockbuster scale.
venues.anecdote_story: The Temple of Mithras was discovered in 1954 during post-Blitz excavations and reconstructed as part of Bloomberg's European headquarters.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Roman Temple of Mithras reconstruction; Bloomberg SPACE art programme. GalleriesNow lists Anne Ryan: Folkmoot from 16 Jul 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Bank/Cannon Street area is safe and central; avoid late empty-lane dispersal.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 7 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Timed-entry capacity; current art exhibition starts 16 Jul 2026, after run date.

venues.opening_hours: Official page lists Mon-Fri 10:00-17:00, last entry 16:30; third Thursday lates until 20:00, booking recommended; closed weekends and bank holidays; closure flags include Friday 3 July from 15:00, Friday 17 July, Friday 28 August from 15:00.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free to visit; lates booking recommended. Group walk-in tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely manageable on weekdays; more didactic than art-led.
venues.capacity_notes: Museum infrastructure is robust; weekday-only limits PAINTS scheduling.
venues.why_fits_paints: The content is more money/history than PAINTS craft, but it has enough object culture and City specificity to work as a lower-risk museum backup. It is particularly useful when nearby galleries are fully booked.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum tells the history of the central bank and its role in the economy; current social posts reference Building the Bank tours.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Money, gold, bank history and current museum events/exhibitions.
venues.safety_notes: Directly at Bank with strong transport links; weekday office crowds improve safety.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 5 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Closed weekends/bank holidays; category fit is weaker than art/craft galleries.

venues.venue_name: St Bartholomew's Hospital Museum and North Wing
venues.opening_hours: Barts North Wing page states from Oct 2025: every Monday & Tuesday 10:00-16:00 and first Sunday of each month 10:00-16:00, free entry, no booking required.
venues.walk_in_policy: Official page says free entry, no booking required; group of 8-12 tolerance UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for small guided-looking self-led visit if hosted during limited opening windows.
venues.capacity_notes: Very limited days; hospital setting requires sensitive hosting.
venues.why_fits_paints: A rare place where medical history, restored architecture and Hogarth murals meet. It is more distinctive than many larger museums and gives attendees a story they are unlikely to know already.
venues.anecdote_story: Guardian reported in Oct 2025 that restored Hogarth murals at St Bartholomew's opened to the public after nearly 300 years largely out of view.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Hospital museum, Great Hall / North Wing setting, William Hogarth murals The Pool of Bethesda and The Good Samaritan.
venues.safety_notes: Smithfield/Farringdon is safe by day; hospital environment demands clear meeting point and respectful visitor behaviour.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 9 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Limited opening days; current-exhibition claim is restoration-led rather than a conventional rotating art show.

venues.opening_hours: Official homepage lists Tue-Sat 10:00-17:00 and Sun 12:00-16:00; Art Fund listing differs, so host must confirm current hours.
venues.walk_in_policy: Museum entry appears visitor-led; group of 8-12 and any timed/ticket requirement UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Small historic house; 8 is safer than 12 unless pre-arranged.
venues.capacity_notes: House museum scale; may feel cramped if other visitors present.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is not a visual-art powerhouse, but it is a strong literary-object museum in a hidden City square. It works best when framed around language, dictionaries and London conversation culture.
venues.anecdote_story: The house preserves the world of Samuel Johnson, the 18th-century man of letters associated with the Dictionary of the English Language.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Historic interiors, Johnson/Dictionaries material, talks; recent London Craft Week event was time-limited in May 2026.
venues.safety_notes: Gough Square is tucked away but central; keep meeting/dispersal on Fleet Street.
venues.backup_venue: St Bartholomew's Hospital Museum and North Wing
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 9 min walk - host must verify route on Google Maps.
venues.risks_watchouts: Small-house capacity and weaker current-exhibition support keep it Tier B.

venues.opening_hours: Official site says the market is open seven days to pass through and explore, while shop opening times vary; public areas are described as open 24/7 in official social post.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access covered market; individual shop/restaurant walk-in policies vary and are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm if using a seated stop.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a short market/architecture wander; weaker for producer-led browsing.
venues.capacity_notes: Covered public market passage with shops, pubs and restaurants; not a classic farmers/craft market.
venues.best_time_slot: Saturday or weekday 10:30-12:00 for architecture before lunch crowds.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Victorian ironwork and Roman/medieval market history are excellent, but the current offer is more shopping/dining than maker market. Include only as a City-specific covered-market fallback.
venues.anecdote_story: Official history dates the market to 1321 and places it in the centre of Roman London; it was originally a meat, poultry and game market.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Covered market architecture, shops, restaurants, pubs and historic lanes.
venues.safety_notes: CCTV and central public access are noted by the official site; strong daytime safety.
venues.backup_walk_time: Approx. 15 min by transit/walk combination from Bank/Liverpool Street - host must verify; outside district but closest stronger market backup.
venues.risks_watchouts: Not producer/maker-led enough for Tier A; very obvious Harry Potter / photo-tour traffic.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Bookshop in district, but no on-site or next-door independent coffee partner found; Waterstones-style chain/retail patterns nearby do not satisfy PAINTS Books & Coffee.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Host Cafe at St Mary Aldermary
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Beautiful independent cafe in a Wren-attributed church, but I found no substantive bookshop/books-on-premises component; coffee-only venues are explicitly disqualified.
Atmospheric and story-rich, but not on the PAINTS-approved Premium or Safety-First park list; Creative Walks may not use it.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: St Dunstan in the East Church Garden
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Photogenic garden/ruin, but not on the PAINTS-approved park/route list; may be considered only if the PAINTS team adds it after safety review.
Not open on run date: official page says the new Smithfield museum opens 28 November 2026.
Operationally a wholesale meat market rather than a curated public maker/food market for warm 8-12 person PAINTS browsing.

Independent and characterful, with official walk-ins welcome, but it is a gin/rum/whisky cocktail bar rather than an independent wine bar, natural-wine pub or character gastro pub under the strict v6 drinks rule.
Good sustainable wine offer but a multi-site brand with official page highlighting work events/private dining; closed weekends per official FAQ, reducing PAINTS launch flexibility.
Hotel/private-members-club ecosystem; PAINTS v6 says no hotel bars/cafes unless exceptional independent heritage/design-led case, and this is too polished/corporate for the brief.
Shopping-centre / mall environment, explicitly outside PAINTS aesthetic and market rules.
Historically strong, but sightseeing hours vary by date and it is a church/heritage visit rather than a museum/gallery day with a current exhibition justifying the event.
High-quality historic venue but geocodes/addresses to Charterhouse Square in Islington/Clerkenwell rather than City of London; also the richest access is tour-led rather than walk-in.
